articles.title: Anthropic says it’s about to have its first profitable quarter
articles.why: Anthropic telling investors it's about to post its first profitable quarter — with Q2 revenue projected at $10.9 billion, more than double its prior run rate — is a landmark moment for the AI industry's most credible safety-focused lab. Profitability changes everything about its independence and leverage.
articles.hook: Anthropic is about to turn its first profit / $10.9 billion in revenue / The safety lab is now a real business.

articles.title: Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25B per month for compute
articles.why: Anthropic paying xAI $1.25 billion per month for compute — rival companies locked in a financial dependency that neither has publicly explained — is one of the most counterintuitive business arrangements in tech history. The SpaceX IPO filing made it undeniable.
articles.hook: Anthropic pays Elon Musk $1.25 billion a month / For compute / The AI rivals are financially entangled.

articles.title: Trump admin didn't want Ebola-exposed Americans, sent them to Berlin, Prague
articles.why: The Trump administration reportedly blocking Ebola-exposed Americans from returning home — forcing allies to take them — is a genuinely shocking policy decision during an active WHO-declared health emergency. This is the day's most alarming Ebola development.
articles.hook: America wouldn't take its own Ebola patients / Germany and Czech Republic did / That's a new sentence.

articles.title: ‘Perfect Storm’: How Trump’s Aid Cuts Are Fueling the Ebola Outbreak
articles.why: The Trump administration reportedly refusing to repatriate Ebola-exposed Americans — sending them to Berlin and Prague instead — is a significant escalation on a story the briefing has been tracking. This is new, specific, and alarming enough to warrant attention on its own terms.
articles.hook: Ebola-exposed Americans weren't allowed home / The White House said no / Berlin and Prague took them.

articles.title: Chance The Rapper Spends $150K on Gordon Parks Reprint Photo at Foundation Gala
articles.why: Chance the Rapper spending $150K on a Gordon Parks photo at the Foundation's anniversary gala is a meaningful act of cultural stewardship — an artist using his wealth to anchor a legacy that matters to Black American visual history.
articles.hook: Chance spent $150K on a Gordon Parks photo. / At the man's own foundation gala.

articles.title: You can now remix other people’s YouTube Shorts with AI
articles.why: YouTube Shorts letting users insert themselves into other people's videos using Gemini Omni is a meaningful escalation in AI-powered creation — and raises real questions about consent, identity, and what 'remixing' means when the tool can replace you with someone else.
articles.hook: YouTube Shorts now lets you insert yourself into anyone's video / AI does it in seconds.

articles.title: Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software is creeping into Europe
articles.why: Tesla's Full Self-Driving creeping into Europe — first the Netherlands, now Lithuania — is a quiet but significant regulatory development. Europe's safety standards are among the world's strictest, and FSD gaining a foothold there signals either a regulatory shift or a very careful market entry strategy.
articles.hook: Tesla's Full Self-Driving is entering Europe / Country by country / Regulators haven't stopped it yet.

articles.title: A Bipartisan Amendment Would End Police License Plate Tracking Nationwide
articles.why: A single line in a highway bill that would effectively ban police license plate tracking nationwide — already covered as an FBI story — is a meaningful legislative escalation on one of the year's live civil liberties debates.
articles.hook: One sentence in a highway bill / Could end license plate tracking nationwide.

articles.title: I Gave My OpenClaw Agent a Physical Body
articles.why: A writer giving a physical robotic body to an AI coding agent — using off-the-shelf tools — is a first-person demonstration of how quickly the barrier between software agents and physical robots is collapsing.
articles.hook: He gave his AI agent a body / It already knows how to code.

articles.title: Hypershell X Ultra S Review: The Best Exoskeleton Yet
articles.why: A consumer exoskeleton that powers your legs without making you feel like a puppet is a small but real benchmark in wearable assistive tech — the kind of product that signals a category is maturing.
articles.hook: An exoskeleton that finally feels natural / Not science fiction anymore.
